name: Digital Product Passport (DPP)
thesis: Is the EU Digital Product Passport becoming real on schedule - on the regulator's side, and on industry's side?

scores.regulator_delivery_note: Of everything the EU committed to deliver by now, how much landed - and on time. Computed over milestones that are due only; at launch the one item past its date (the service-provider act) slipped, so the score sits at 0.

this_week_read.headline: Six of eight DPP standards are out. The one act that was already due slipped.
this_week_read.body: CEN-CENELEC published the first six DPP system standards (EN 18216 to 18223) on 27 May 2026; the final two are under approval, expected September. None are cited in the Official Journal yet, so the legal presumption of conformity is still pending - you can build against these standards, but you cannot yet make compliance claims on them. Meanwhile the only deliverable already past its date, the delegated act setting requirements for DPP service providers, remains a draft rather than an adopted act. The registry's 19 July 2026 set-up deadline still stands on paper, but the implementing rules that make it operational only closed consultation on 27 May - putting a registry that actually accepts registrations at risk of slipping into later 2026.

milestones.label: DPP Registry operational and accepting registrations
milestones.note: Set-up deadline is hard; the implementing regulation that makes it operational closed consultation 27 May 2026.

milestones.label: DPP system standards published (EN)
milestones.note: EN 18216-18223 published 27 May 2026; FprEN 18239 and one further standard under approval, expected September.

milestones.label: Standards harmonised (cited in the Official Journal)
milestones.note: Publication is not harmonisation. OJ citation, which creates the presumption of conformity, is still pending.

milestones.label: Customs interconnection live
milestones.note: Four years after the registry rules enter into force.

milestones.label: DPP service-provider delegated act adopted
milestones.note: Still a draft delegated act (requirements for DPP service providers plus a conformity-assessment scheme); not adopted.

milestones.label: Iron and steel ESPR delegated act adopted
milestones.note: First priority product group; not yet adopted, timelines drifting.

milestones.label: Textiles, tyres, aluminium ESPR acts adopted
Mattresses and ICT ESPR acts adopted
Energy-related product rules updated
milestones.note: Rolling range, tracked as ongoing progress.

signals.name: Active DPP pilots across value chains
Public DPP solutions / vendors live
signals.source: Market mapping / CIRPASS-2 Community of Practice
signals.note: ~15 established public DPP solution vendors (Circularise, Kezzler, Scantrust, Spherity, OPTEL, Protokol and peers); the CIRPASS-2 Community of Practice now lists 111 participants.

signals.name: GS1 Digital Link adoption proxy
signals.note: GS1 Sunrise 2027 - the 2D / Digital Link migration most DPP carriers ride on - is in pilot across 48 countries representing 88% of world GDP.

signals.name: Implementable standards (published as EN)
signals.note: Of the 8 JTC 24 areas, how many are stable enough to build against today.

calls.statement: The EU DPP Registry will be operational and accepting registrations by its legal deadline of 19 July 2026.
The DPP service-provider delegated act will be adopted by 30 June 2026.
All eight JTC 24 DPP system standards will be published as European Standards by 30 September 2026.
At least one DPP standard will be cited in the EU Official Journal (presumption of conformity) by 31 December 2026.
The iron and steel ESPR delegated act will be adopted within 2026.
The textiles ESPR delegated act will not be adopted before 2027.

methodology: Track A scores the EU's own delivery against the dates the regulation set, over milestones that are due only, weighted (registry and the service-provider act count most). delivered_on_time = 1.0, delivered_late = 0.5, slipped/missed = 0, upcoming is excluded until its date passes. Track B is a provisional proxy of industry readiness and is tagged as such until the proprietary onboarding signal replaces the basket. Calls are falsifiable, dated, and graded HIT or MISS in public, with a running win-rate.

source.line: Sources: Regulation (EU) 2024/1781 (ESPR); European Commission DPP FAQ v1.0 (DG GROW); CEN-CENELEC JTC 24; EUR-Lex. Verified 24 June 2026.
